history.historicalImportance: Arwal is an important district of the Magadh region. The district has historical, cultural and agricultural significance and is situated on the banks of the Son River.
history.formationHistory: Arwal was carved out of Jehanabad district and became an independent district on 6 August 2001 to improve administrative efficiency and regional development.
history.freedomMovement: The people of Arwal participated in India's freedom movement through the Non-Cooperation Movement, Civil Disobedience Movement and Quit India Movement. Many local freedom fighters contributed to the national struggle.
history.archaeologicalImportance: The district has several ancient temples, historical sites and archaeological remains reflecting the cultural heritage of the Magadh region.
historicalTimeline: Ancient period – The region formed part of the ancient Magadha Kingdom.
Medieval period – Came under the rule of the Delhi Sultanate and later the Mughal Empire.
1765 – The region came under the administration of the British East India Company.
1947 – Became part of independent India after Independence.
6 August 2001 – Arwal became a separate district after being carved out of Jehanabad district.

geography.location: Arwal district is located in the south-central part of Bihar in the Magadh region. The Son River forms an important geographical feature of the district.
geography.climate: Subtropical climate with hot summers, monsoon rains and cool winters.

specialEconomicFeatures: Strong irrigation from the Son river system

agriculture.dairyDevelopment: Dairy farming is an important source of rural livelihood in Arwal district.
agriculture.fisheries: Fish farming is practiced in ponds, canals and other inland water bodies.

infrastructure.roadNetwork: Arwal district has a developing road network connecting all blocks and nearby districts.
infrastructure.airConnectivity: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan International Airport, Patna (about 70 km).
infrastructure.publicTransport: Bihar State Road Transport buses, private buses, taxis and auto-rickshaws.
infrastructure.electricity: Most villages and urban areas have electricity connectivity under state and central rural electrification schemes.
infrastructure.waterSupply: Water supply is provided through piped water schemes, hand pumps and tube wells.
infrastructure.internetConnectivity: 4G and 5G mobile networks are available, along with broadband services in urban areas.

education.governmentSchools: More than 600 government schools are functioning across the district.
education.privateSchools: Several private schools affiliated with CBSE and Bihar School Examination Board operate in Arwal.
universities: No university is located in Arwal district. Colleges are affiliated with Magadh University, Bodh Gaya.
medicalColleges: No medical college in the district

healthcare.primaryHealthCentres: Primary Health Centres are functioning in all major blocks of the district.
medicalColleges: No medical college in the district
healthcare.ambulanceServices: 102 and 108 ambulance services are available throughout the district.
notableHospitals: Community Health Centre, Sonbhadra Banshi Suryapur

cultureAndHeritage.traditionalDress: Men traditionally wear Dhoti-Kurta and women wear Saree and Salwar-Kameez.
historicalHeritage: Ancient temples of the Son river region
culturalOrganizations: District Art and Culture Office, Arwal

wildlifeSanctuaries: No wildlife sanctuary within the district

sportsAchievements: District athletes regularly participate in state-level competitions.
School and college teams represent Arwal in Bihar-level tournaments.

bankingAndFinance.atms: ATMs of major public sector and private sector banks are available across Arwal district.
bankingAndFinance.digitalBanking: UPI, Internet Banking, Mobile Banking and AEPS services are widely available.
financialInclusionPrograms: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana

governanceAndPublicServices.districtAdministration: The district is administered by the District Magistrate (DM), Arwal.
governanceAndPublicServices.policeAdministration: Law and order is maintained by Arwal Police under Bihar Police.
governanceAndPublicServices.fireServices: Fire stations operate in Arwal district under Bihar Fire Services.
governanceAndPublicServices.publicDistributionSystem: Essential commodities are distributed through the Public Distribution System (PDS) across the district.
eGovernanceServices: Bihar Right to Public Services Portal

lawAndOrder.policeDistrict: Arwal Police District under Bihar Police
lawAndOrder.trafficPolice: Traffic Police regulates traffic and road safety across Arwal district.
lawAndOrder.disasterManagement: The District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), Arwal coordinates disaster preparedness, flood response and emergency relief.

mediaAndCommunication.postalServices: India Post provides postal, Speed Post and parcel services across Arwal district.
mediaAndCommunication.telecommunication: 4G and 5G mobile connectivity is available across most parts of the district.

stateRankings: Important agricultural district of South Bihar
Known for paddy and pulse cultivation
nationalRankings: Part of the Magadh region of Bihar
notableAchievements: Growth in digital governance and public services
records: Arwal is one of Bihar's smallest districts by area.

famousPersonalities: Chandrashekhar Singh (Former Chief Minister of Bihar)
importantDates: 6 August 2001 – Arwal became a separate district
15 August 1947 – Became part of independent India
additionalInformation: Arwal is one of the youngest districts of Bihar.
Agriculture is the main occupation of the district.
The Son River plays an important role in the district's economy.
